message:
feat: Add SLO tracking, DOM assist summaries, and seam marker persistence

This release introduces Service Level Objective (SLO) monitoring for capture and OCR performance,
comprehensive DOM assist event tracking, and enhanced seam marker persistence with hash counts.
The implementation provides better visibility into performance bottlenecks and quality metrics
through both CLI tools and the live capture UI.

## Core Features
- **SLO Tracking**: Implement p99 performance budgets for capture and OCR operations
  - Calculate budgets as 2x p95 values for each metric category
  - Track violations with visual indicators (⚠️) when p99 exceeds budget
  - Persist SLO status in weekly summaries for trend analysis

- **DOM Assist Summaries**: Add comprehensive DOM assist event aggregation
  - Collect and categorize assist reasons (low-alpha, punctuation, etc.)
  - Track frequency counts per reason with sample data
  - Display summaries in both CLI warnings tail and live capture UI

- **Seam Marker Persistence**: Extend database schema for seam tracking
  - Add seam_marker_count and seam_hash_count columns to RunRecord
  - Calculate unique hash counts from seam marker arrays
  - Persist counts for efficient querying and aggregation

## CLI Enhancements
- **scripts/mdwb_cli.py**: Add DOM assist column to warnings table
  - Format DOM assist summaries with reason counts and samples
  - Display assist statistics in warning log tail output
  - Augment warning records with dom_assist_summary_text field

- **scripts/show_latest_smoke.py**: Display SLO metrics in weekly summaries
  - Show capture SLO status with p99 vs budget comparison
  - Add OCR SLO tracking when OCR metrics available
  - Format SLO violations with warning indicators

- **scripts/run_smoke.py**: Calculate SLO budgets and p99 metrics
  - Extract OCR timing from manifest timings block
  - Compute p99 percentiles for capture, total, and OCR metrics
  - Generate SLO blocks with budget calculations and status flags

## Database & Storage
- **app/store.py**: Extend RunRecord with seam tracking columns
  - Migrate schema to add seam_marker_count and seam_hash_count
  - Apply manifest metadata to extract seam counts and unique hashes
  - Clone seam data when duplicating run records

- **app/warning_log.py**: Add DOM assist summary generation
  - Implement summarize_dom_assists() for event aggregation
  - Track reason frequencies with Counter
  - Include sample DOM assist entries in warning logs

## Web UI Updates
- **web/app.js**: Render DOM assist summaries in live view
  - Add renderDomAssistSummary() for real-time display
  - Show assist counts, reasons, and frequency tables
  - Display sample DOM text with reason categorization

- **web/tailwind.css**: Style DOM assist panel components
  - Add dom-assist-panel with dark theme styling
  - Style dom-assist-table with bordered layout
  - Match seam-table visual hierarchy

## Testing
- **tests/test_show_latest_smoke.py**: Test SLO display and calculations
  - Verify SLO status indicators for over-budget scenarios
  - Test capture and OCR SLO formatting
  - Ensure weekly summary includes SLO blocks

- **tests/test_mdwb_cli_warnings.py**: Test DOM assist display
  - Verify DOM assist summary in JSON output
  - Test pretty-print formatting with reason counts
  - Ensure augmented fields include dom_assist_summary_text

- **tests/test_store_manifest.py**: Test seam marker persistence
  - Verify seam_marker_count extraction from arrays
  - Test unique hash count calculation
  - Ensure backward compatibility with direct count fields

## Documentation & Planning
- Update PLAN_TO_IMPLEMENT_MARKDOWN_WEB_BROWSER_PROJECT.md
- Track implementation progress in bead_status.txt
- Update plan_note.txt with current milestone

## Operational Improvements
- Better performance visibility through SLO tracking
- Enhanced debugging with DOM assist reason analysis
- Improved seam marker metrics for quality assessment
- Comprehensive warning log enrichment for troubleshooting

This release significantly improves observability and quality metrics tracking,
providing operators with detailed insights into capture performance, OCR reliability,
and DOM-based recovery patterns. The SLO framework establishes clear performance
baselines and automatically flags degradations for investigation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 - _2025-11-08 — BlueMountain (bd-805) opened to implement the low-confidence OCR detectors + DOM text overlays so headings/captions can be patched automatically when OCR struggles._
 - _2025-11-09 — BlueMountain (bd-805) delivered the first hybrid recovery pass: low-confidence heuristics now patch Markdown with DOM text (manifest `dom_assists`, event + CLI diag output, and highlight links for quick inspection)._ 
+- _2025-11-09 — PinkCastle (bd-805 follow-up) added spaced-letter/code-fence guards plus manifest/SSE summaries so CLI/UI panels show DOM-assist counts, reason buckets, and sample text without cracking manifests._
 - Detect low-confidence OCR regions (symbol rate, low alpha ratio, hyphen density) and patch them with DOM text overlays scoped to the offending block (hero headings, captions, icon fonts).

+
+
+def summarize_dom_assists(entries: Sequence[Any] | None) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
+    if not entries:
+        return None
+    normalized: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    for entry in entries:
+        if entry is None:
+            continue
+        if isinstance(entry, Mapping):
+            normalized.append(dict(entry))
+        elif is_dataclass(entry):
+            normalized.append(asdict(entry))
+    if not normalized:
+        return None
+    counter = Counter(str(item.get("reason", "unknown")) for item in normalized)
+    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
+        "count": len(normalized),
+        "reasons": sorted(reason for reason in counter if isinstance(reason, str)),
+        "reason_counts": [
+            {"reason": reason, "count": count} for reason, count in counter.most_common()
+        ],
+    }
+    sample = next((entry for entry in normalized if entry.get("reason")), normalized[0])
+    summary["sample"] = {
+        "tile_index": sample.get("tile_index"),
+        "line": sample.get("line"),
+        "reason": sample.get("reason"),
+        "dom_text": sample.get("dom_text"),
+    }
+    return summary

@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@ bd-l9n Granian runtime toggle + docs — DONE (RedDog 2025-11-09): `scripts/run_

-bd-805 Hybrid text recovery heuristics — DOM low-confidence detectors (replacement-char, punctuation bursts, mixed numeric, hyphen-break) now drive DOM overlays in `stitch_markdown`, manifest `dom_assists`, SSE/NDJSON events, CLI diag/watch tables, and the `/jobs/{id}/artifact/highlight` helper.
+bd-805 Hybrid text recovery heuristics — DOM low-confidence detectors (replacement-char, punctuation bursts, mixed numeric, hyphen-break) now drive DOM overlays in `stitch_markdown`, manifest `dom_assists`, SSE/NDJSON events, CLI diag/watch tables, and the `/jobs/{id}/artifact/highlight` helper. 2025-11-09 — PinkCastle added spaced-letter + code-fence guards plus the dom_assist summary surfaced through manifest/SSE/CLI/UI (manifest tab shows counts/reasons live).
 bd-90m OCR concurrency autotune & HTTP/2 tuning — DONE (RedDog 2025-11-09): adaptive limiter in `app/ocr_client.py`, manifest/SSE/CLI surfacing (`ocr_autotune`), UI + docs updates, and regression tests ensure scaling decisions + telemetry stay stable.
 bd-rle Rich CLI E2E logging scaffolding — DONE (PurpleBear 2025-11-09): FlowLogger v2 + transcript exporter + the `mdwb fetch --resume --webhook` scenario now live in `tests/test_e2e_cli.py` (panels/tables/progress + artifact logs).
 bd-rlc Agent + warnings integration coverage — DONE (PurpleBear 2025-11-09): `tests/test_e2e_cli.py` now covers `agents summarize` + `agents generate-todos` with transcript exports plus the combined `mdwb warnings tail --json` → `mdwb diag --json` flow, asserting on `rich` Panels/Tables/Progress + artifact paths.

@@ -32,5 +32,6 @@ Starting bd-x6v cache reuse work now - GreenMountain
 2025-11-09 — PinkCastle (bd-b9e follow-up) audited the stitcher DOM assist/table trim paths: fixed hyphen-break overlays to merge multi-line headings, taught table trimming to skip blank/comment prologues, and added regression tests + run_checks/ruff/ty.
 
 2025-11-09 — PinkCastle (bd-b9e follow-up) tightened DOM overlay handling again: list/blockquote prefixes are preserved, overlay candidates are consumed in FIFO order so duplicate headings don’t reuse stale DOM text, and the warnings CLI table now folds overly long cells (plus the sweep summary prints both the precise overlap ratio and a truncated variant so the existing smoke tests still see `ratio=0.9`).
+2025-11-09 — PinkCastle (bd-805) wired the new dom_assist summaries end-to-end: manifests/SSE expose reason counts + samples, the CLI renders the aggregated tables, and the UI manifest tab now shows assist totals with live updates when SSE dom_assist events arrive.
 2025-11-09 — PurpleBear picked up bd-hki (generative E2E guardrail tests) to review PLAN §14 requirements, outline the generated scenario harness, and prep the remaining fixtures/docs before coding.
 2025-11-09 — PurpleBear (bd-hki) upgraded `tests/test_e2e_generated.py` with FlowLogger output, diff snippets, and README/docs toggle notes so the generative guardrail suite is actionable whenever `MDWB_RUN_E2E_GENERATED=1` is set (override cases via `MDWB_GENERATED_E2E_CASES`).

@@ -1500,6 +1505,28 @@ def _format_blocklist(values: Any) -> str:
     return ", ".join(parts)
 
 
+def _format_dom_assist_summary(summary: Any) -> str:
+    if not isinstance(summary, Mapping) or not summary:
+        return "-"
+    count = summary.get("count")
+    reasons = summary.get("reasons") or []
+    reason_counts = summary.get("reason_counts") or []
+    if isinstance(reason_counts, list) and reason_counts:
+        formatted_counts = ", ".join(
+            f"{entry.get('reason', 'unknown')}({entry.get('count', '')})" for entry in reason_counts
+        )
+    elif reasons:
+        formatted_counts = ", ".join(str(reason) for reason in reasons)
+    else:
+        formatted_counts = "-"
+    sample = summary.get("sample") or {}
+    sample_reason = sample.get("reason")
+    parts = [f"{count} assist(s)" if count is not None else "assists", formatted_counts]
+    if sample_reason:
+        parts.append(f"sample={sample_reason}")
+    return " | ".join(part for part in parts if part and part != "-")
+
+
 def _format_sweep_summary(record: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
     stats = record.get("sweep_stats")
     if not isinstance(stats, dict):
